summary: Anthropic 推出了 Managed Agents，把 agent 基础设施和业务逻辑解耦了；同时 Claude Code 默认切到 xhigh effort，还做了一次更偏 usage 的升级。Rauch 说，agent 需要的是持久性，不是花哨 prompt。Swyx 则把 AI engineering 分成了 slop 和 rigor。

builderInsights.title: Alex Albert
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Opus 4.7 更可预测，也更精致了
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，Opus 4.7 在 async work、instruction-following 和 token control 上明显更强了。它还新增了 xhigh effort level，让输出更可预测。另一个变化是，它不再把高分辨率图片降采样了。UI、slides 和 docs 也更有审美了。这类体验上的升级，才是真正会影响日常 workflow 的东西。

tweets.text:
Some of my favorite things in Opus 4.7:
- Very good at async work and following instructions
- Effort levels are far more predictable for token control (+ new xhigh level)
- No more downscaling of high-res images
- Noticeably more taste in UIs, slides, docs https://t.co/GlTiMfwFVa

builderInsights.title: Cat Wu
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Claude Code 现在默认用 xhigh effort
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他们说，Claude Code 在 Opus 4.7 上现在默认设成 xhigh 了。你也可以用 `/effort` 把它调高或调低。更关键的是，model 现在更会自己检查改动了，所以他们在推动 teams 把 testing workflow 写进 `claude.md`，或者做成一个 `/verify-app` skill。

tweets.text: Excited to centralize our updates in one place! https://t.co/mKdwedB5TU

We've set the default effort level for Opus 4.7 to xhigh in Claude Code. You can use /effort to adjust this. 

Excited for you to try Claude Code with Opus 4.7 and let us know your feedback!

3. Tell the model how to verify its changes. Put your testing workflow in your claude.md, or add a /verify-app skill. Opus 4.7 is better at verifying it's work, and it's helpful to share any local dev tips that are hard to discover.

builderInsights.title: Guillermo Rauch
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Agent 需要的是 durability，不只是聪明的 prompt
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，agent 和 backend 真正难的地方是 durability：model 会挂，API 会限流，database 会卡住，但你还是会被 pager。它的核心观点是，Workflow SDK 给 backend app 带来的可靠性，就像 Next.js 之前给 frontend dev 带来的那种体验，而且从第一天起就支持 self-hosting 和 multi-cloud。

tweets.text:
The hardest thing about agents and backends is durability. @workflowsdk fixes this.

That LLM you're calling *will* go down. That service *will* rate limit you. That database *will* unexpectedly slow down. You *will* get paged 💀

I've been looking for a unicorn for a decade. I wanted the level of reliability of combining stuff like SQS / Kafka / microservices, and I absolutely did not want *that* at the same time 😂

Truly reliable systems like that are notoriously difficult to reason about, to develop locally, to test, to simulate, to deploy… Workflow SDK solves that without compromises. 

We're doing what Next.js did for the frontend, but for one of the most important problems of the new generation of backend applications. 

Notably, Workflow SDK has an incredible self-hosting and multi-cloud story from day 0. We've taken amazing lessons from Next.js and poured them into the many Worlds (adapters) you can deploy to.

Congrats to Pranay and the Workflow team on a generational ship: https://t.co/ub7vQ7L6yE

The rate of progress in AI is relentless. You can capture the upside of its volatility with @aisdk and @vercel AI Gateway.

Congrats to @anthropicai on another banger ship, but @xai, @openai, and @googleai are coming. Gonna be a fun year. https://t.co/tRPh2lgL8S

builderInsights.title: Amjad Masad
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Web app 可以用不到 $10 变成 iOS app
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，你可以花不到 $10，把一个 web app 直接变成 iOS app。这种分发技巧，才是 founder 真正会在意的。其他内容基本都是 Replit 的 promo：parallel agents 更快运行，打 5 折，还有 EU deployment。但核心信号还是一样：一次发布，低成本覆盖 mobile。

tweets.text: You can turn your web app into an iOS app for less than $10 https://t.co/3BGIKIYIHT
50% off -- especially useful to run parallel agents and make faster progress on your project! https://t.co/Z6jBeM5wV6
Deploy to EU! https://t.co/jIIqnuI1Sc

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
builderInsights.title: Aaron Levie
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Codex 把企业内容变成 agent 燃料
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 新的 Codex 又往前推了一步。它让 agent 不只是会写代码，还能用工具、在后台跑长任务，帮知识工作者干活。他说，Box plugin 让这件事尤其强，因为它能把企业内容在各个 app 之间流起来，自动化处理报告、data room、合同、入职和发票这些事。

tweets.text:
The new Codex is another jump in what agents will look like for knowledge workers. Agents that can code, work with tools, and use computers, can begin to execute long running tasks in the background for all areas of work. 

This can mean drafting reports, setting up data rooms for a merger, reviewing contracts, helping onboard clients, generating marketing assets, processing invoices, and more. 

With the Box plugin inside of the new Codex, you can begin to automate almost any kind of work with enterprise content. And importantly, being able to work across multiple apps is a huge point of leverage because we can now far more easily connect our tools together.

builderInsights.authorBio: Design @Cursor_ai. Early @NotionHQ, @Stripe, built startups. I make a world where anyone can make software. Aspiring k-pop idol.
builderInsights.title: Ryo Lu
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Cursor 的 design stack 正在变成一款独立产品
builderInsights.suggestedSummary:
他说，不同任务用不同 model：规划用 Opus 4.7，搭建和迭代用 Composer 2，处理那些最难缠的 bug 用 Codex/GPT-5.4。这个信号很明确：Cursor 正在把 model choice 变成 workflow design，而不只是一个带 autocomplete 的 chat box。

他还预告了 Baby Glass。它是一个新的 prototyping environment，和 @flowstated 一起做的。设计师可以用 code 重组想法，而且用的还是支撑 Cursor 3 interface 的那套 shared components。

builderInsights.title: Kevin Weil
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: OpenAI 推出面向真实实验室的 science model
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他们发布了 GPT-Rosalind，一个针对 biology、drug discovery 和 translational medicine 调优的 frontier model，还内置了研究人员实际会用到的数据库和工具知识。因为 bio-safety 风险，他们只给符合条件的客户开放访问；同时也把一个 Life Sciences plugin for Codex 推给了所有人。

tweets.text: The new codex computer use is shockingly good https://t.co/2nW3jSVI46

See our blog post for videos and demos: https://t.co/YDE0XkFuUO

Request access to GPT-Rosalind here: https://t.co/XqrW4n4Lmg

Life Sciences plugin: https://t.co/m9JHIgLqNg

💥 Super excited to launch GPT-Rosalind, our first frontier model built for scientific research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. 

The model is trained in chemistry, protein engineering, genomics and more, including built-in knowledge of relevant databases and tools researchers use.

We want to make these capabilities available to scientists and researchers while maintaining strong safeguards against biological misuse. GPT-Rosalind is launching through a trusted access deployment structure for qualified customers. 

We're also launching a Life Sciences plugin for Codex to everyone today, which works with our mainline models as well as GPT-Rosalind.

The 3 things that are the best use of my time:

builderInsights.authorBio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox
builderInsights.title: Peter Yang
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI workflows 需要第二个 agent 来给第一个打分
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，最实用的做法是再起一个独立的 eval agent，专门对另一个 agent 的输出做简单的 yes/no 检查。然后让它一直跑，直到全部通过。他已经把这套模式用在 YouTube thumbnails 和 titles 上了。这也很直观地说明，AI 工作流正在从一次性 prompt，变成 agent 对 agent 的 QA。

tweets.text: Claude and Codex apps should build an easy way to view and edit markdown files from web and mobile if I want to hand craft the last 10% of instructions

When I build skills now I always ask AI to spin up a separate eval agent to do yes/no checks to grade the first agent's output.

And if the output isn't "yes" across the board it asks the first agent to keep working.

Building one for YouTube thumbnails and titles now. https://t.co/UvOoxekYVy
Why do any real work when you can just migrate from openclaw -> hermes -> perplexity computer -> openclaw again

podcastHighlights.title: Notion 的 Token Town：5 次重构、100+ 工具、MCP vs CLIs，以及软件工厂的未来——Notion 的 Simon Last 和 Sarah Sachs
podcastHighlights.suggestedTitle: Notion 押注 agent，但前提是模型先配得上
podcastHighlights.suggestedSummary:
**核心结论：** Notion 的优势不在 hype，而在于知道什么时候该等，什么时候该重构，什么时候该先发出去再说。

- 他们把 custom agents 重构了四五次，因为模型当时“太笨”，context 也太短；耐心比过早打磨更值钱。
- 他们真正的优势不是纯 AI 能力，而是产品判断力：不去“逆流而上”，同时盯着河流什么时候改道。
- 公司把 agents 当成未来的 interface，所以现在每个 product team 都得同时为人和 agent 设计，而不是只在现有产品上硬贴一层 AI。

Notion 的 cofounder Simon Last 和负责大部分 AI org 的 Sarah Sachs 讲的是一支从 2022 年底就开始死磕 agents 的团队。早期尝试失败，是因为 tool calling 当时根本还不成熟。后来即使能用了，可靠性也还是不够支撑 background work。真正的突破来得更晚。但教训不是“等更强的 model”，而是学会读懂时机：能力不够时先往前做，但别在死胡同里硬撞。

Sarah 的说法最到位：工作一边是别让公司“逆流而上”，另一边是提前为水流改道做准备。这也体现在 Notion 做 AI 的方式上。他们不迷信 hackathon，但会拿它来扩散团队的 fluency。他们也不靠自上而下拍脑袋，而是让好奇的 builder 做出来的 prototype 变成真正的 product。至于 evals，他们也不当成官僚流程，而是专门搭了一个“agent dev velocity” org，让各团队自己负责测试，同时还能安全地持续 ship。

最后形成的文化就是："demos over memos" 不是口号，而是操作系统。Notion 的赌注是，software factory 的未来不会来自一个巨型 agent，而是来自一堆小而精、可观测、能嵌进现有产品里的 agent，而这些产品本身已经被用户信任。

blogUpdates.title: Scaling Managed Agents: 将 brain 和 hands 解耦
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: Anthropic 发布 Managed Agents，把 agent infrastructure 解耦
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary:
导语：Anthropic 推出了 Managed Agents。这是一个托管在 Claude Platform 上的 service，用来跑 long-horizon agents。它带有 durable session log、stateless harness 和 isolated sandbox。这样系统就能持续演进，不用把整套 stack 重新接线。

数据：
- p50 time-to-first-token 下降了大约 60%。
- p95 time-to-first-token 下降了 90% 以上。
- 这个架构支持 many brains 和 many hands。tools 通过一个很简单的 `execute(name, input) -> string` interface 暴露出来。

重点：对 builder 来说，最大的变化是架构层面的。Claude 的“brain”不再必须和它的 tools、state 或 credentials 放在同一个 container 里。这让 reliability 更好，debug 也终于可做了。团队还可以把 agent 接到外部 infrastructure，比如 VPC，而不用把所有东西都塞进一个 box 里。安全性也更强了，因为 tokens 不会留在 sandbox 里，access 会通过 vault-backed proxies 或 bundled auth 来走。Anthropic 想传达的核心意思是：harness 的假设会很快过时，因为 model 一直在进化。原话是："We expect harnesses to continue evolving." Managed Agents 的定位，就是给这层演进提供一个稳定底座，同时还支持 context recovery、retries，以及通过 MCP 接 custom tools。

blogUpdates.title: Claude Managed Agents：让生产落地快 10 倍
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: Claude 推出 Managed Agents，加速 production deployment
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary:
**Lead：** Claude Managed Agents 现已进入 public beta。它提供可组合的 API 和托管基础设施，让你在几天内就能搭建、运行和治理 cloud agents，而不是拖上几个月。

**Numbers：**
- 官方说法是能让团队把 agents 的交付速度提升 “10x faster”
- 内部测试里，针对结构化文件生成的任务，成功率比标准 prompting loop 最高提升了 10 个百分点
- 长时间运行的 session 可以持续几个小时，哪怕中途断线也没问题
- 一些客户集成原本要几个月，最后几周就上线了；还有一个团队在一周内就部署了 specialist agents

**So What：** 做 agent 的团队可以直接跳过生产化里最麻烦的部分：sandboxing、checkpointing、permissions、auth、tracing 和 orchestration。这样就能把精力放在用户体验上。Claude 会负责 agent harness、tool use、context 和 recovery。Console 则提供 session tracing、integration analytics 和 troubleshooting。这个平台还在 research preview 里支持 multi-agent coordination，也允许团队在 autonomous execution 和更紧的 prompt-and-response control 之间做选择。原文说得很直接："You define your agent’s tasks, tools, and guardrails and we run it on our infrastructure." 对于要上线 coding、productivity、finance 或 legal agents 的团队来说，这意味着更快发布，也少了很多自建 infra 的活。
